harmonized_determinants.definition: Video treatment designed to induce anger about climate change.
raw_labels_grouped: Anger-inducing climate video (vs no video) – progressive carbon tax
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Experimental treatment, not a trait measure.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Video treatment designed to induce fear about climate change.
raw_labels_grouped: Fear-inducing climate video (vs no video) – progressive carbon tax
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Experimental treatment, not a trait measure.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Video treatment designed to induce positive affect or relaxation.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Experimental treatment, not a trait measure.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Support changes when the same price instrument is labeled differently.
raw_labels_grouped: Republicans preferred to pay more... labelled as a carbon offset than... labelled as a carbon tax (no difference for Democrats)
Tax-label aversion: carbon tax vs carbon offset wording
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Labeling/framing effect, not a substantive policy change.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Information video explaining how climate policies work.
raw_labels_grouped: Information video explaining how climate policies work
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Experimental information treatment.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Information video on climate-change impacts.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Experimental information treatment.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Combined information treatment using climate-impacts and policy videos.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Experimental multi-arm treatment.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Exposure to a pros-and-cons framing about the policy.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Experimental framing treatment.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Making distributional impacts of the policy salient.
raw_labels_grouped: information on its distributional properties are made salient
Salient information on progressive distributional impacts
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Treatment about distributive consequences.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Information or salience treatment about revenue recycling.
raw_labels_grouped: salience and communication of revenue recycling
Salience and information about revenue recycling
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Information treatment, not the recycling design itself.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Satisfaction with how the policy is explained or communicated.
raw_labels_grouped: Satisfaction with governmental information provision about the policy

harmonized_determinants.definition: Understanding of the policy's rules or functioning.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: May reflect objective or self-reported comprehension.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Knowledge of climate science or climate-change facts.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes self-reported and objective knowledge measures.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Self-rated knowledge of climate-policy design or details.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Subjective knowledge, not objective understanding.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Incorrect beliefs about policy costs, benefits, or comparative alternatives.
raw_labels_grouped: Most people underestimate the benefits of lower emissions and overestimate drawbacks such as job losses
Cost misperceptions about taxes reduce support
overestimate the costs of taxes and underestimate the ‘hidden’ costs of alternative policies
Misperceived costs/benefits (underestimate gains, overestimate job losses) reduce support
pessimistic bias about net gains (overestimate losses)
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Excludes actual household cost exposure.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Associations of the policy or climate issue with skepticism, politics, generic climate imagery, or uncertainty.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Image or framing associations, not substantive beliefs.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Association of the issue with skepticism or denial.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Image/label association rather than a belief measure.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Association of the issue with alarmist or catastrophic imagery.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Image/label association rather than a stable trait.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Neutral or generic climate-change image association.
Association of the issue with politics or partisan framing.
Uncertainty or lack of a substantive response.
Changes in acceptability after exposure, implementation, or staged rollout.
raw_labels_grouped: The carbon tax in British Columbia became acceptable over time
Acceptance increases once the policy is enacted, so implementing a tax gradually is more successful
existing carbon pricing policy in place
calls to ‘axe the tax’ faded once residents received rebates on income tax
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes implementation learning and post-enactment adjustment.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Deliberative engagement that increases support.
raw_labels_grouped: a majority of participants favoured a carbon tax after deliberation
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Deliberation is treated as learning/experience, not a demographic trait.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Affective worry, anxiety, or negative emotional response to global warming.
raw_labels_grouped: Holistic negative affect about global warming
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Some studies use repeated or inverse-coded measures.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Concern about climate risk, seriousness, or urgency.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes inverse-coded low-concern measures.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Belief that climate change is real and human-caused.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Covers both general and human-causation belief.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Rejection or low acceptance of climate science.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from low concern or uncertainty.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Desire for stronger political action on climate change.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: General climate-policy orientation, not tax-specific.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Belief that the tax or policy reduces emissions, pollution, or related outcomes.
raw_labels_grouped: Believing that the tax is effective (environmental effectiveness)
environmental effectiveness of carbon tax & dividend
Believes the policy would reduce GHG emissions
Believes the policy would reduce air pollution
were more likely to support a fuel tax if they believed the tax was going to reduce driving and exert a positive effect on climate change
Perceived effectiveness best predicted people’s policy choices... more effective... than a carbon tax
Belief that the carbon tax is ineffective
Believing that the tax is effective (environmental effectiveness)
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes inverse-coded effectiveness beliefs.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Expected broader co-benefits beyond emissions reduction.
raw_labels_grouped: Believes the policy would have positive econ. effects
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from core emissions-reduction beliefs.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Belief that pricing pollution works through Pigouvian incentives.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Theory-based efficacy belief.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Perception that viable low-carbon substitutes are unavailable or too costly.
raw_labels_grouped: alternatives are insufficient or too expensive
Perceived lack of affordable alternatives to fossil fuels
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Often supports carbon-tax acceptance.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Confidence in government, public institutions, or politicians.
raw_labels_grouped: Political trust emerges as an important factor
trust in government handling of carbon tax revenues
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes revenue-use-specific trust when not separated in the study.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Confidence in scientists and scientific institutions.
raw_labels_grouped: trust in university and IPCC scientists
trust in environmental scientists and environmental organizations
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Separate from trust in government or industry.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Confidence in the fossil-fuel industry as a source or actor.
raw_labels_grouped: trust in industry negatively associated with greater policy support
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Often negatively associated with tax support.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Belief that the tax is a hidden device to raise revenue.
raw_labels_grouped: Suspicion that the carbon tax is a pretext to raise taxes
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Trust deficit specific to tax motives.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Party identification, vote choice, or partisan leaning.
raw_labels_grouped: Trump voter (vs Harris voter) – progressive carbon tax
partisan leaning / vote choice (Biden vs Trump)
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Keep separate from general ideology.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Affiliation with green, ecologist, or left-green political identities.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from general left-right ideology.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Left-right, conservative, market-liberal, anti-intervention, or hierarchical worldviews.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Combines ideological and worldview scales, not party ID.

harmonized_determinants.definition: General value priorities, including pro-environmental and egalitarian values.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from party identity and ideological orientation.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Attention to or engagement with politics.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Political engagement rather than ideology.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Alignment with anti-tax protest movements.
raw_labels_grouped: Support for the Yellow Vests movement
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Specific movement affiliation, not a general ideology.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Perceived social support, pro-environmental norms, or responsibility cues.
raw_labels_grouped: Environmental organizations in successfully spreading support for British Columbia’s carbon tax
support for a carbon tax falls when people receive information that only a minority of other citizens support the proposed tax
Feelings of personal responsibility to reduce climate change
Personal responsibility to reduce climate change
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes descriptive norms, advocacy cues, and responsibility norms.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Direct exposure to fuel-tax costs through driving, vehicle ownership, or commuting mode.
raw_labels_grouped: Car-dependent respondents are less supportive of taxes on fossil fuels and carbon taxes with cash transfers
Commute mode 'other (public transit, bike, carpool)' (reference 'drive myself')
more frequent public transit use increases Tax & dividend approval
diesel vehicle ownership increases opposition to diesel tax
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Measures actual or expected exposure, not fairness judgments.

harmonized_determinants.canonical_label: Personal financial burden and expected net losses
harmonized_determinants.definition: Expected household cost, purchasing-power loss, or net personal loss from the tax.
raw_labels_grouped: Perceived private cost to own household
Higher energy prices and less purchasing power
belief that one does not lose from it (win/lose category)
Subjective expected net gain (implied WTP threshold)
WTP from subjective gain (subjective net purchasing-power change)
perceptions that climate policies are in one’s self-interest
South Africans preferred the revenues to be shared as a dividend, because this would do more to relieve the economic burden of a carbon tax there
harmonized_determinants.recommended_main_table_label: Personal financial burden and expected net losses
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from misperceptions and from fairness concerns.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Exposure through employment in affected sectors.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Sectoral or occupational rather than household-level exposure.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Concern that the tax harms sectoral competitiveness or jobs.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from individual household cost exposure.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Overall judgment that the tax or policy is fair.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Generic fairness, not specific distributional incidence.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Perceptions that the tax burden falls unfairly on low-income, rural, or other disadvantaged groups, or that it is regressive or progressive.
raw_labels_grouped: perceived distributional fairness of carbon tax and revenue use
Carbon pricing policies can be regressive…
Perceived regressivity and burden on low-income households
Believes the carbon tax would penalize the poorest
Belief the carbon tax would penalize the poorest reduces approval
Believes the high-income earners will lose
Neutral cost distribution (all citizens pay the same share of income)
Regressive incidence (all citizens pay the same amount; effects on the poor)
Progressive incidence (higher-income citizens pay a larger share of income)
Perceived rural distributional harm from the carbon tax
Concern with income inequality (Swiss 2015 energy tax ballot)
Concern with income inequality (1 = at least some, 0 = no or little concern)
Progressivity belief (benefit the poorest / not regressive)
distribution of costs is progressive... richer members... pay a larger share and potential revenues are redistributed
Preference for progressive burden-sharing increases acceptability
people tend to like ‘progressive’ policies under which costs are borne mainly by those most able to pay
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Covers burden on specific groups and broader incidence perceptions.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Endorsement of fairness principles such as equality, need, or polluter-pays.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Normative fairness principles, not perceived fairness of a specific tax.

harmonized_determinants.definition: A tax schedule that rises with income or thresholds, shifting burden toward higher-income groups.
raw_labels_grouped: Progressive carbon tax design protecting low-income households
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Design feature, not a perception or belief.

harmonized_determinants.definition: The nominal level of the carbon or fuel tax.
raw_labels_grouped: Higher tax rate / much higher tax rate of the green tax reform
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from household cost exposure.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Policy seen as preserving consumer choice and autonomy.
raw_labels_grouped: Policy does not limit freedom of choice
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Liberty framing rather than price or revenue effects.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Preference for subsidies or subsidy reform over direct CO2 taxation.
raw_labels_grouped: people approved ... subsidies ... or expansion of public transportation. To a significantly lesser degree they supported two types of CO2 taxation
Prefer pull measures over coercive CO2 taxation
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Instrument-choice framing, not revenue use.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Framing the policy around redistributing or recycling revenues.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Framing treatment, not an actual transfer.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Settlement context from rural to urban.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Individual residence and municipality-size measures.

harmonized_determinants.canonical_label: Specific place, language, and world-region context
harmonized_determinants.definition: Country, canton, province, language, or world-region context.
raw_labels_grouped: aggregated region (Global North versus Global South)
Global South vs Global North (tax support)
harmonized_determinants.recommended_main_table_label: Specific place, language, and world-region context
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Aggregate world-region, country, province, canton, and language measures.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Local environmental pollution or exposure context.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Contextual exposure, not policy design.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Macro or regional economic context affecting affordability.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes regional income, unemployment, and energy-price context.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Access to or use of transport alternatives to driving.
raw_labels_grouped: improved public transport can reduce some public opposition to gasoline (petrol) taxes
More frequent public transit use increases Tax & dividend approval
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Often proxies lower fuel-tax exposure.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Individual female sex or gender, relative to male reference.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from aggregate female shares.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Individual age or age-category differences relative to a reference group.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Covers specific brackets and older/younger contrasts.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Individual educational attainment or area-level higher-education share.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes individual attainment and area-level education shares.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Individual or household income and related nonlinearity terms.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from regional GDP per capita.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Individual racial identity, here white/Caucasian versus reference groups.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Demographic control.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Area-level demographic composition such as age, sex, or education shares.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Contextual demographics, not individual characteristics.

harmonized_determinants.canonical_label: General climate and policy interaction by world region
harmonized_determinants.definition: Interaction between world region and ideology, trust, or values.
raw_labels_grouped: trust in public institutions × aggregated region
Biospheric values × Global region (tax support moderation)
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Moderation terms, not main effects.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Using tax revenue for environmental or climate-related projects.
raw_labels_grouped: earmarking carbon tax revenues ... for environmental projects or other issues
Environmental earmarking of carbon tax revenues
support a rise in fossil fuel taxes if revenues are earmarked... whereas without earmarking... prefers a decrease
Earmark revenues for mitigation projects in all countries/worldwide
Funding mitigation projects worldwide received the highest support (65%), on average.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from household rebates or tax cuts.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Channeling revenue to green investment, research, infrastructure, or public services.
raw_labels_grouped: Subsidies to low-carbon technologies and energy efficiency
Funding technological innovation and energy efficiency
Mitigate negative impacts of climate change
fund environmental infrastructures, subsidize low-carbon technologies, or reduce income taxes
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Project types vary across studies.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Returning revenue to households through rebates, transfers, or dividends.
raw_labels_grouped: Targeted transfers (social cushioning) to low-income and vulnerable households
Uniform lump-sum transfers (carbon dividends) to all households
cash transfers to the poorest households / cash transfers to constrained households
equal cash transfers to all households
Equal share of revenue given to each taxpayer
redistributing revenues domestically to each citizen
Sharing out funds per capita globally also appealed ... but not in the United States (44%).
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes universal, targeted, and equal-share schemes.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Returning revenue to industries or sectors harmed by the tax.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from household compensation.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Recycling revenue through reductions in other taxes.
raw_labels_grouped: Reducing indirect taxes, such as VAT
Using revenues to reduce indirect taxes (e.g. VAT)
Reducing labour taxes (including personal income taxes and social contributions)
Using revenues to reduce labour taxes
Using revenues to lower domestic income taxes
Revenue recycling to taxpayers (VAT/income tax reductions)
tax cuts – income, labour, and consumption
tax cuts for income, labour, and consumption taxes
reduction in the VAT rate (61% approval)
Use carbon tax revenue to reduce VAT increases acceptance
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Includes income, labour, VAT, and consumption tax cuts.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Using revenue to cut corporate taxes.
raw_labels_grouped: Revenue recycling to corporate tax cuts
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Separate because support can differ from household tax cuts.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Allocating revenue to debt reduction or the general budget.
raw_labels_grouped: Use carbon tax revenue to reduce the government deficit
supplementing the government budget / general budget
Using revenues to supplement the general government budget
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from earmarked spending or household compensation.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Belief that carbon-tax revenue is returned so net government revenue is unchanged.
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Belief measure rather than actual spending option.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Sharing revenues internationally on an equal-per-capita basis.
raw_labels_grouped: Sharing out funds per capita globally also appealed ... but not in the United States (44%).
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Distinct from domestic dividends and country-targeted funds.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Whether climate revenues are shared with all countries or only developing or emerging countries.
raw_labels_grouped: allocate climate-fund revenues to all countries vs only emerging/developing
could succeed if its revenues were allocated to all countries. Transferring wealth from developed to only emerging countries might trigger opposition in the former
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Cross-country distributive design.

harmonized_determinants.canonical_label: Revenue control under harmonized carbon taxes
harmonized_determinants.definition: Global carbon-tax design where countries keep control over revenue use.
raw_labels_grouped: A global system of harmonized carbon taxes, in which countries retain control over the revenues
harmonized_determinants.recommended_main_table_label: Revenue control under harmonized carbon taxes
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Institutional design, not earmarking choice.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Support based on both expected effectiveness and progressive incidence.
raw_labels_grouped: broadly perceived effectiveness ... distributive effect ... richer people fly more
perceived effectiveness and progressivity explain support for kerosene tax
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Bundles efficacy and distributional arguments.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Ideological reactions to revenue-return design or burden neutrality.
raw_labels_grouped: carbon taxes do not swell government coffers, which appeals to the political right
The left is also interested because the average tax burden is unchanged and low-income households are better off
Political-right appeal when revenues are returned (no swelling government coffers)
Political-left appeal when dividends keep average burden neutral and help low-income
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Revenue recycling moderates partisan support.

harmonized_determinants.definition: Experimental omission that lowers salience of price impacts.
raw_labels_grouped: without mentioning the impact on prices
harmonized_determinants.distinction_notes: Treatment, not an inherent cost exposure.
